steps.title: Identify what the passage emphasizes
steps.content: The passage opens by stating that women like Evelyn Marie Harper made important early contributions to cryptology, then details Harper's specific achievements, and closes by noting how Harper and others helped more women — such as Dr. Nia Patel — enter the field. The central point is that women have contributed to cryptology, historically and today.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the right one
steps.content:
Test each choice against the passage's central focus on women's contributions to cryptology:
  • Choice A — ‘Cryptology should be taught more often in schools to encourage more women to enter the field.’: the passage does not argue for a policy change or make any recommendation about school curriculum. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Women such as Evelyn Marie Harper and Dr. Nia Patel have contributed to the field of cryptology.’: directly matches the passage's opening claim and the evidence provided throughout. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Cryptology is a field that focuses primarily on securely managing data.’: this is a definition mentioned briefly in passing, not the main idea of the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Evelyn Marie Harper and Dr. Nia Patel worked together on an important project in the field of cryptology.’: the passage never states these two women worked together; Patel is introduced only as an example of a woman who entered the field later. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
